hard.ais_why:
• Sole Avoma meeting (23 Apr 2026) is explicitly titled 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins' ‚Äî the meeting title is itself the strongest possible buyer-AI-interest signal. Recording was disabled (missing conferencing details) so no transcript-level notes are available, but the title plus 4 Clarins attendees (Alice Levan, Emmanuelle Wallon, Remi Belle, plus the opp owner) confirms AI-focused dialogue was the meeting's purpose.
• Qualified stage at $200K. Product scope has ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake plus a broad S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but LastActivity 2026-04-24 indicates recency. Multiple named stakeholders engaged. The opp is a major French luxury cosmetics buyer; AI in luxury procurement is a strategic positioning play.
• Medium confidence: the explicit 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins' meeting title and stakeholder breadth support AI Hungry status, but the absence of transcript-level evidence prevents High confidence. Score 7 reflects the AI-titled meeting + 3 AI SKUs in scope + multi-stakeholder attendance; would push to 8-9 with a transcript confirming buyer-spoken AI use cases.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / do-nothing (no S2P in place)

north_star_verdict.headline: Greenfield S2P at family-owned Clarins with a strong multi-threaded procurement champion (Alice Levan), but the September CPO meeting and the whole win hinge on a quantified business case the buyer has not yet built; the buyer pushed the July prep dates on 24 Jun, so the one near-term action has stalled buyer-side.
north_star_verdict.math: 256 days to close (2027-03-19) from today 6 Jul 2026. Created 31 Mar 2026, ~97 days old, in Qualified the entire cycle; no submission-date fields populated so velocity is read from creation and Next_Step history. Last buyer touch 1 Jul 2026 (rep email to Alice awaiting reply; buyer emails 24-25 Jun). Buyer-stated pace: RFP release Aug/Sep 2026, selection Q1 2027, close aligns with a 6-8 week post-selection contracting window. Near-term milestone (use-case prep meeting, targeted 3rd week July) slipped when the buyer said on 24 Jun they were not ready and would revert. No forward CloseDate slip since the single 27 Apr move (2026-12-31 to 2027-03-19). Best Case forecast is not defensible pre-RFP with no engaged economic buyer and no quantified ROI; recommended Pipeline.
evidence: Last buyer engagement: rep email to Alice Levan 1 Jul 2026 chasing use-case slots; buyer emails 24-25 Jun 2026 (photos exchange plus use-case coordination). Deal is live but rep-side awaiting reply.

Buyer pushed the proposed prep dates on 24 Jun 2026: 'Nous ne serons pas prets aux dates proposees, nous reviendrons vers vous' (Alice, task email 24 Jun) — the July prep meeting is buyer-gated and has slipped.

CPO Emmanuelle Wallon (Decision Maker contact role, inferred Economic Buyer) last engaged at the 23 Apr 2026 on-site demo, ~10 weeks ago; a September meeting is proposed but gated on business-case strength (Next_Step 25 Jun).

No quantified business problem, savings target or ROI case in Salesforce or Next_Step; Alice Levan tasked with defining internally 'the best business case(s) / use case(s) that would convince Emmanuelle' (Next_Step 25 Jun).
4 Clarins procurement staff attended the Zycus AI Paris Tour 16 Jun 2026 (Next_Step 21 May, 15 Jun 'Good meeting during HA days'), showing active multi-threaded engagement and AI interest.
Buyer-stated timeline: project validation Q2 2026, RFP Q3/Q4 2026, selection Q1 2027, flagged 'need to be validated that's the ideal planning' (Next_Step 27 Apr).
Full S2P suite in scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA); Amount raised 200K to 300K on 21 May (field history).

SAP Ariba named in Salesforce Competitors__c; no active competitive evidence in Next_Step, tasks or (empty) call transcripts. Clarins runs no S2P today, so status-quo / no-decision is the primary threat.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, savings target, cycle-time or ROI case has been articulated by Clarins or captured in Salesforce. The Next_Step history (25 Jun, 15 Jun, 21 May) repeatedly circles the need to define and validate 'a clear business case' and 'the most relevant B Case' to present to CPO Emmanuelle Wallon and 'convince the family Clarins (Family Courtin-Clarins owning Clarins)', but no financial figures exist. Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais are tasked with defining two use cases internally before the July prep meeting (email 24 Jun: 'nous pourrions valider ensemble un ou deux uses cases par exemple sur le suivi des economies par categorie'). Quantifying savings by procurement category, Merlin AI efficiency and supplier/contract risk reduction is the single highest-leverage move before the Aug/Sep RFP.
sources: Next_Step 25 Jun 2026: 'need before to have a clear business case to present to ensure she will understand the value of Zycus.'
Task email 24 Jun 2026 (Pierre to Alice, Marilyn): 'afin de convaincre Emmanuelle, nous pourrions valider ensemble un ou deux uses cases par exemple sur le suivi des economies par categorie'
Next_Step 21 May 2026: 'work with them on B Case to ensure it will be strong enough to convince the family Clarins (Family Courtin-Clarins owning Clarins)'

economic_buyer.narrative: Emmanuelle Wallon, Chief Procurement Officer (OpportunityContactRole Decision Maker), is the economic buyer: as CPO of a family-owned house she controls the procurement budget and is the person the entire business-case effort is built to 'convince' (Next_Step 25 Jun, 15 Jun). She is mapped but not engaged: her only direct touch was the 23 Apr 2026 on-site demo, and re-engagement is deferred to a proposed September meeting that is itself gated on the business case landing. Final validation sits above her with the Courtin-Clarins family owners (Next_Step 21 May), so even CPO sponsorship is not the last gate. The move is to secure her September slot and land a quantified value case directly with her before the RFP, converting assumed sponsorship into active sponsorship.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Emmanuelle Wallon, Chief Procurement Officer, role = Decision Maker
Next_Step 25 Jun 2026: 'Proposition of a meeting with Emmanuelle Wallon CPO in September'
Next_Step 21 May 2026: 'convince the family Clarins (Family Courtin-Clarins owning Clarins)'

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation will run through a formal RFP the buyer plans to release in Aug/Sep 2026 with selection targeted Q1 2027 (Next_Step 27 Apr, 21 May), so the criteria will be codified in that cahier des charges — not yet shared. What is visible today is that the buyer weights AI/agentic capability heavily (the 23 Apr meeting was explicitly titled 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins' and 4 procurement staff invested a day at the 16 Jun AI Paris Tour) and wants concrete, category-level use cases they can defend internally (email 24 Jun: two use cases 'les plus pertinents vs vos differentes offres'). Confirmed: AI relevance and use-case fit. Inferred/unknown: the formal scoring criteria, weightings and must-haves that will appear in the RFP. Shaping those requirements before the RFP opens is the lever.
sources: Next_Step 27 Apr 2026: 'RFP in Q3/Q4 for a selection in Q1 2027'
Task email 24 Jun 2026 (Alice): 'identifier les 2 use cases les plus pertinents vs vos differentes offres'
Event 23 Apr 2026 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins'; Next_Step 21 May 2026 AI Paris Tour attendance

decision_process.narrative: The process reads clearly from the activity sequence: an initial CNA gala introduction, an on-site demo on 23 Apr 2026, then a widening procurement coalition (the 16 Jun AI event drew Alice Levan, Marilyn Bordais, Mickael Sarkissian, Julie Belin Declerck, Antoine Saouma), an internal use-case definition step led by Alice and Marilyn, a proposed September CPO meeting, a formal RFP in Aug/Sep, selection in Q1 2027, and contracting to a 19 Mar 2027 close. The approval chain runs procurement team to CPO Emmanuelle Wallon to the Courtin-Clarins family owners (Next_Step 21 May). The process is buyer-controlled and pre-RFP, which is why long gaps between formal events are normal here rather than a stall; the risk is that the internal business-case step, which the whole chain depends on, is the current bottleneck.
sources: Salesforce activities 31 Mar - 1 Jul 2026 (on-site demo 23 Apr, AI event 16 Jun, use-case emails 24-25 Jun)
Next_Step 27 Apr 2026: 'Validation of this project in Q2 / RFP in Q3/Q4 for a selection in Q1 2027'
Next_Step 21 May 2026: family-owner validation required

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible yet, which is expected for a Qualified, pre-RFP deal 256 days from close: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec or partner involvement has surfaced in any field or activity. The one paper-relevant forcing function is the buyer's own RFP, expected Aug/Sep 2026, which will define the formal procurement and evaluation path. Remi Belle (Finance & HR DPO) is on the account and signals a future data-privacy/DPA touchpoint given the AI modules in scope. Visibility will come from the RFP itself and from asking the buyer, once the business case is validated, to share their sourcing and contracting timeline in writing.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Remi Belle, Finance & HR DPO
Next_Step 27 Apr / 21 May 2026: RFP Q3/Q4 2026 as the formal procurement gate
No MSA/SOW/InfoSec references in any Salesforce field or activity

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed qualitatively but not quantified. Clarins runs no Source-to-Pay platform today (a greenfield estate), and the buyer is running a structured reflection toward an S2P selection, which is the operational pain: fragmented, manual procurement across categories at a global luxury house. The AI angle is a stated priority (the AI-titled 23 Apr meeting and 4 staff at the 16 Jun AI event), pointing to a desire for automation and AI-driven category savings. What is missing is the buyer's own articulation of the cost of the status quo in numbers, which is precisely what Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais have been asked to define. The pain owner is the procurement organisation under CPO Emmanuelle Wallon.
sources: Next_Step 21 May / 25 Jun 2026: internal work to define use cases 'sur le suivi des economies par categorie'
Prior record: 'Pas de solution de Source2 Pay en place actuellement, reflexion en cours, cahier des charges a venir avant l'ete' (17 Apr)
Products in scope: full S2P suite plus Merlin AI and ANA

champion.narrative: Alice Levan, Global Sustainable & POS Procurement Manager (OpportunityContactRole Evaluator), is a confirmed, active champion. She has been the entry point since 31 Mar 2026, coordinated the 23 Apr on-site CPO demo, drove attendance of 4 Clarins procurement staff at the 16 Jun AI Paris Tour ('So clearly we have not only Alice as champion but also the key procurement people', Next_Step 21 May), and is co-leading internal use-case definition with Marilyn Bordais. Her advocacy is real and multi-threaded, and she engaged as recently as 24-25 Jun. Her one limit: her championship is process-gated — she owns an internal business-case step she has not yet completed (she said on 24 Jun they were not ready and would revert), and her access to the CPO is confirmed but she has not yet converted it into active CPO sponsorship. The test is whether the case she builds is strong enough to convince Emmanuelle Wallon and the family owners.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Alice Levan, Global Sustainable & POS Procurement Manager, role = Evaluator
Next_Step 21 May 2026: 'we have not only Alice as champion but also the key procurement people'
Task email 24 Jun 2026 (Alice): 'Nous devons nous concerter avec Marilyn et Mickael... nous reviendrons'

competition.narrative: There is no active rival in the room. Clarins runs no S2P platform today, so the real contest is winning internal validation against status-quo / no-decision inertia and the scrutiny of the Courtin-Clarins family owners, not beating a named vendor. SAP Ariba is listed in Salesforce Competitors__c but carries no supporting buyer evidence in any call, email or Next_Step, so it is treated as a dormant, RFP-time possibility rather than a live threat. The wedge is Zycus's broad S2P suite plus agentic AI (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA), which is already resonating (the AI-titled demo and the well-attended 16 Jun AI event). We win by converting that AI resonance into a quantified, category-level business case the CPO can defend internally, and by shaping the Aug/Sep RFP requirements before any competitor is invited in.
sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: SAP Ariba (no corroborating buyer evidence)
Prior record 17 Apr 2026: 'Pas de solution de Source2 Pay en place actuellement'
Next_Step 21 May 2026 AI Paris Tour resonance; Event 23 Apr 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins'

deal_movement.summary: Live, buyer-controlled pre-RFP engagement. Since May the coalition widened (4 procurement staff at the 16 Jun AI event) and the focus moved to internal use-case / business-case definition; the buyer pushed the proposed prep dates on 24 Jun and the rep chased again on 1 Jul. Amount rose 200K to 300K (21 May) and forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case; close moved once (27 Apr, 2026-12-31 to 2027-03-19) and has held since.

On-site demo delivered with the CPO present ('Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins')

competitive_position.summary: Status-quo / no-decision is the single strongest CURRENT threat: Clarins operates no S2P today and the project must still clear internal business-case validation with CPO Emmanuelle Wallon and the Courtin-Clarins family owners before any RFP (Aug/Sep 2026). SAP Ariba is named in Salesforce but shows no active buyer evidence in any call, email or Next_Step, so it ranks as a dormant, RFP-time possibility, not a live rival. Zycus's wedge is the full S2P suite plus agentic AI (Merlin/ANA), already resonating via the AI-titled 23 Apr demo and the 16 Jun AI event.

champion_strength.summary: Alice Levan is a strong, active, multi-threaded champion: she opened the account, coordinated the 23 Apr CPO demo, drove 4 procurement staff to the 16 Jun AI event, and co-leads internal use-case definition with Marilyn Bordais, engaging as recently as 24-25 Jun. She has confirmed CPO access. What is still developing is conversion: she owns an internal business-case step she has not completed (said on 24 Jun they were not ready), and CPO sponsorship is not yet active. Not at risk, but her advocacy will be tested by whether the case she builds convinces Emmanuelle Wallon and the family owners.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole Evaluator; Next_Step 31 Mar, 27 Apr, 21 May, 15 Jun, 25 Jun; Task emails 24-25 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic-AI story (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) is landing at procurement-team level: the 23 Apr demo was AI-titled and 4 staff invested a day at the 16 Jun AI Paris Tour. But it is under-positioned where it counts, it has not been converted into a quantified, CPO-facing business case, and the economic buyer has not seen the AI value story since April.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate (AIS_Status__c). The AI-titled 23 Apr meeting, 3 AI SKUs in scope and 4 procurement staff at the 16 Jun AI event point to genuine AI appetite trending toward AI Hungry; the ceiling on confidence is the absence of any transcript-level buyer-spoken AI use case.

items.detail: No quantified savings or ROI case exists at ~97 days in; a weak business case will not survive CPO and Courtin-Clarins family-owner scrutiny.
Economic buyer / CPO Emmanuelle Wallon not engaged since 23 Apr; sponsorship assumed, and final validation sits above her with the family owners.
Greenfield with no S2P today; the primary threat is internal inertia / no-decision, meaning Clarins keeps managing procurement as-is and never validates the project.

items.detail: Buyer pushed the proposed July prep dates and the whole path depends on a buyer-owned internal step; the Aug/Sep RFP and Q1 2027 selection could slip if the business case lags.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and multi-threaded but paced entirely by the buyer, who just pushed the near-term prep dates; engagement is at procurement-manager level, not CPO. Not cooling, but momentum is buyer-gated on the business case.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Greenfield S2P at family-owned Clarins with a strong multi-threaded procurement champion (Alice Levan) and real AI pull, but no engaged economic buyer and no quantified business case yet; the whole win hinges on landing a defensible use-case case before the Aug/Sep RFP.

win_position.text: Qualified stage caps confidence: it earns a mid read on a strong multi-threaded champion (Alice Levan) and genuine AI pull (4 procurement managers at the 16 Jun AI Paris Tour), held down because it is pre-RFP with no business case.
No economic-buyer engagement since the 23 Apr on-site demo: CPO Emmanuelle Wallon's sponsorship is assumed not confirmed, and she must still convince the Courtin-Clarins family owners.
No quantified savings or ROI case exists ~3 months in, and the buyer said on 24 Jun they were not ready and would revert, so the near-term prep meeting has already slipped.

deal_momentum.text: Active multi-threaded engagement through June: Alice, Marilyn Bordais and Mickael Sarkissian coordinating use cases, buyer emailing 24-25 Jun and the rep chasing again on 1 Jul.
The buyer controls the pace and pushed the proposed prep dates on 24 Jun ('nous ne serons pas prets aux dates proposees'); the RFP is not due until Aug/Sep, so the clock is entirely buyer-owned.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer named a concrete timeline (RFP Aug/Sep 2026, selection Q1 2027) and 4 procurement staff invested a full day at the 16 Jun AI event, signalling real project intent.
Commitment is procurement-level only: the CPO and family owners have not endorsed the project, and no commercials, pricing or RFP have started.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing / no-decision is the primary threat: Clarins runs no S2P today and the project still needs internal validation by the CPO and the family-owned board.
SAP Ariba is named as a competitor in Salesforce but shows no active buyer evidence; it could resurface once the Aug/Sep RFP opens.

deal_risk.evidence: Greenfield no-S2P inertia plus an unvalidated business case and family-owner approval gate.

customer_commitment.evidence: Concrete buyer timeline and 4-person AI-event attendance, but no exec endorsement or commercials.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised 200K to 300K on 21 May 2026 with the full S2P plus Merlin AI suite in scope; scope is broadening, not narrowing.

critical_signals.text: No price, savings or ROI case exists yet; the buyer must first build internal use cases to justify the project to the Courtin-Clarins family owners.
Since the well-received 23 April on-site demo the CPO has stayed out of the room and the buyer pushed back on proposed prep dates on 24 June, keeping momentum at procurement-manager level.

Marilyn Bordais (Media & Marketing Procurement) and Mickael Sarkissian (IT/Digital) are now actively co-defining use cases alongside champion Alice Levan, a widening and engaged procurement coalition.
No active rival in the room: Clarins runs no S2P today so the real contest is winning internal validation before the Aug/Sep RFP, with SAP Ariba named but showing no live activity.

customer_preference.evidence: Strong AI resonance (AI-titled demo, 4 procurement staff at the 16 Jun AI event) and active use-case coordination, but no explicit Zycus-vs-rival preference stated and no commercial next step requested.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings or quantified value articulated; buyer tasked to define internally the use cases 'that would convince Emmanuelle' (Next_Step 25 Jun).

commentary.win_position: Win 23 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): concrete dates (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 8 is anchored on the stage win (23), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Greenfield S2P at family-owned Clarins with a strong multi-threaded procurement champion (Alice Levan) and real AI pull, but no engaged economic buyer and no quantified business case yet; the whole win hinges on landing a defensible use-case case before the Aug/Sep RFP.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Qualified stage caps confidence: it earns a mid read on a strong multi-threaded champion (Alice Levan) and genuine AI pull (4 procurement managers at the 16 Jun AI Paris Tour), held down because it is pre-RFP with no business case.
No economic-buyer engagement since the 23 Apr on-site demo: CPO Emmanuelle Wallon's sponsorship is assumed not confirmed, and she must still convince the Courtin-Clarins family owners.
No quantified savings or ROI case exists ~3 months in, and the buyer said on 24 Jun they were not ready and would revert, so the near-term prep meeting has already slipped.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Qualified' anchors near 18. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 23.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Active multi-threaded engagement through June: Alice, Marilyn Bordais and Mickael Sarkissian coordinating use cases, buyer emailing 24-25 Jun and the rep chasing again on 1 Jul.
The buyer controls the pace and pushed the proposed prep dates on 24 Jun ('nous ne serons pas prets aux dates proposees'); the RFP is not due until Aug/Sep, so the clock is entirely buyer-owned.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Do-nothing / no-decision is the primary threat: Clarins runs no S2P today and the project still needs internal validation by the CPO and the family-owned board.
SAP Ariba is named as a competitor in Salesforce but shows no active buyer evidence; it could resurface once the Aug/Sep RFP opens.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Status-quo / no-decision is the single strongest CURRENT threat: Clarins operates no S2P today and the project must still clear internal business-case validation with CPO Emmanuelle Wallon and the Courtin-Clarins family owners before any…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Buyer named a concrete timeline (RFP Aug/Sep 2026, selection Q1 2027) and 4 procurement staff invested a full day at the 16 Jun AI event, signalling real project intent.
Commitment is procurement-level only: the CPO and family owners have not endorsed the project, and no commercials, pricing or RFP have started.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Draft two category-savings use cases and send Alice for the CPO case. (by 10 Jul)
Book the July prep meeting with Alice and Marilyn to validate the business case. (by 17 Jul)
Secure the September CPO slot with Emmanuelle Wallon anchored on quantified savings and Merlin AI. (by 1 Aug)

calls_omitted.call: Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins (23 Apr 2026)
Invitation Exclusive: Table Ronde sur l'IA Agentique dans le Procurement | Zycus | Paris (16 Jun 2026)
calls_omitted.reason: no transcript / notes available in the manifest

gaps: No readable buyer call transcripts this run (both discovered meetings lack transcripts), so all evidence is from Next_Step, activities/emails, contact roles and the prior record.
No quantified business case / ROI captured.
Formal RFP evaluation criteria and any competitors beyond the field-named SAP Ariba are unknown.
